title: 第 33 節
en: II. If it be asked 'Which are the sixteen Vikaras or evolutions ? ' the answer is, ' the eleven sense organs (including Manas), and the five elements/ rive Budahindriyas.
9-13. ' Now the organs are set forth; the ear, the skin, the eyes, the tongue, and the nose, constitute the five Buddhindriyas, or perceptive organs.

The ear perceives as its object sound, the skin touch, the eye colour, the tongue savour, the nose odour/ Being produced from the Tanm&tras, the senses, as perceiving, are represented as being of the same nature as the objects perceived, a view of considerable antiquity* 252 INDIAN PHILOSOPHY.
PIve Karmendriyas.

14-18. 'The five Karmendriyas or organs of action, voice, hands, feet, the organ of excretion, and the organ of generation, perform each its own work. The voice utters words, the hands work, the feet perform movement, the organ of excretion evacuation, the organ of generation pleasure/ Manas.

19. 'Manas, mind, both perceptive and active, performs its acts of doubting and ascertaining/ Central organ of the senses or KOIVOV alcrOrjTTipLov might be the nearest approach to the meaning of Manas; but mind may do, if we only remember its Samkhya definition, as perceptive, like the other organs, and at the same time active like the Karmendriyas.

'Thus have the eleven organs been explained. Their synonyms are KaraTza, instruments, Vaikarika, changing, Niyata, special, Padani, appliances \ Avadlm^tani, kept under (?), ATMI, atomic, Aksha 2, organ/ rive Mafc&bJmtas.

30-24. 'The Mah&bhutas, or gross elements, are earth, water, light, air, and ether/ Here the earth, we are told, helps the other four, by being their support. Water helps the other four by moistening. Light helps the other four by ripening. Air helps the other four by drying. Ether helps the other four by giving space.

savour, and odour. Water is possessed of four qualities, sound, touch, colour, and savour. Light is possessed of three qualities, sound, touch, and colour. Air is possessed of two qualities, sound and touch. Ether has one quality, sound. Thus are the five MaMbhutas explained.

Their synonyms are : Bhutas, elements, JBhuta-viseshas, special elements, Vik&ras, modifications, Akritis, species,' Tanu, skin (or body ?), Yigraha, shapes, $&nta, pleasurable, 1 Garb©, Sawkhya-PMlosopliie, p. 257. 3 Or Akshara, imperishable?
PTOUSEA. 253 Ghora, fearful, MtU?Aa, stupid. Thus have the sixteen Vikaras been described/ Puruslia.

III. 25. Now it is asked, « What is the Purusha ? ' and the answer is, £ Purusha is without beginning, it is subtle, omnipresent, perceptive, without qualities, eternal, seer, experiencer, not an agent, knower of objects, spotless, not producing. Why is it called Purusha? Because of its being old (Pur&n&t), because it rests in the body (Puri sayate), and because it serves as Purohita (Director).' These are, of course, fanciful etymologies; and we can hardly doubt that we have, in the name of Purusha, a recollection of the Vedic Purusha, one of the many names of the supreme deity, by the side of Visvakarman, Hira^yagarbha, Pra</apati, &c. Like Brahman when conceived as Atman, Purusha also was probably used both for the divine and for the human side of the same power. It is the multiplicity only of the Purusha which is peculiar to the S£mkhya-philosophy.

there is no beginning, no middle, and no end of it.' This is not a very satisfactory answer, but it is probably meant for no more than that we never perceive a beginning, middle, or end of it. Why is it subtle ? Because it is without parts and supersensuous. Why omnipresent ? Because, like the sky, it reaches everything, and its extent is endless. Why perceptive ? Because it perceives (that is, for a time) pleasure, pain, and trouble. Why without qualities ? Because the qualities of good, indifferent, and bad are not found in it. Why eternal ? Because it was not made, and cannot be made. Why seer ? Because it perceives the modifications of Prak?^iti. Why enjoyer? Because being perceptive it perceives (for awhile) pleasure and pain. Why not an agent ? Because it is indifferent and without the qualities (Gtmas). Why the knower of body or of objects ? Because it knows the qualities of objective bodies. Why spotless ? Because neither good nor evil acts belong to the Purusha. Why not producing? Because it has no seed, that is, it can produce nothing. Thus has the Purusha of the S&mkhya been described 254 INDIAN PHILOSOPHY.

The synonyms of Purusha are, Atman, Self, Pum&n, male, Pumgu?ia#antugiva&, a male living creature, Kshetragwa, knower of objects or of the body, Nara, man, Kavi, poet, Brahman, Akshara, indestructible, Pram, spirit, YaAkaA J, anybody, Sa£, He.

Thus have the twenty-five substances been described, viz. the eight Prakritis, the sixteen Vikaras, and the Purusha. He who knows these twenty-five substances, whatever stage of life he may be in, and whether he wear matted hair, a topknot, or be shaven, he is liberated, there is no doubt. This verse is often quoted by Samkhya philosophers. Here, it seems, the first part of the Tattva-samasa is ended, containing a list of the twenty-five Tattvas, in the three divisions of Prakritis, Vikaras, and Purusha.
Purusha (subject).
I 1. Praknti (object). Avyakta (chaos).
2. Mahat or Buddhi (light and intelligence as Samashti, not yet | " individualised).
3. Ahamkara^subjeetivation), 5 Tanmatras (Sattvika) 10 Indriyas, organs (Ra^asa) + i Manas (mind) (subtle elements). (5 Buddhtudriyas, 5 Karmendriyas, and Manas).
Tanmatras. Buddhindriyas. Karmendriyas.
1. Sound, Sabda. i. Srotra, hearing i. Speaking in tongue in ear.
2. Touch, Sparsa. 2. Tvafc, touch in 2. Grasping in hands.
skin.
3. Colour, Rupa. 3. JTakshus, seeing 3. Moving in feet, in eye.
4. Savour, Rasa. 4. <?ihva, tasting 4. Evacuating in Payu.
in tongue.
5. Odour, Gandha. 5. Ghrawa, smell- 5. Generating in TJpastha.
ing in nose.
5 Mahabhutas (Tamasa).
1. Akasa, ether (^abda).
zh: 第二、若有人問：「十六種毘迦羅（Vikaras）或演化形式是哪些？」其答覆為：「十一種感官器官（包括心意），以及五大元素、五種佛陀感根。」
9-13.「現在器官被闡述了；耳、皮膚、眼睛、舌頭和鼻子，構成五個佛智根（*Buddhindriyas*），即知覺器官。
耳朵以聲音為其對象而感知，皮膚以觸覺，眼睛以顏色，舌頭以滋味，鼻子以氣味而感知。這些感官由坦馬特拉（Tanmātras）所產生，作為感知者，它們被理解為與所感知的對象具有相同的本質，這是一個極其古老的觀點。
五種業根（Karmendriyas）。
14-18. 「五種業根（Karmendriyas）或行動器官——音聲、雙手、雙足、排泄器官及生殖器官——各自各司其職。音聲發出言辭，雙手從事勞動，雙足執行運動，排泄器官執行排瀉，生殖器官產生快樂。心意（Manas）。
19. 「心識（Manas）是心靈，既具有知覺性又具有主動性，執行懷疑與確定的作用。感官的中樞器官或 κοινὸν αἰσθητήριον 或許是最接近心識（Manas）意義的說法；然而倘若我們牢記其數論派的定義——作為知覺者如同其他感官般具有知覺性，同時又如業根（Karmendriyas）般具有主動性——「心靈」一詞也是恰當的。
「如是十一個器官已得解釋。其同義語為：迦囉紮（KaraTza）、工具；毗迦瑞迦（Vaikarika）、變化；尼耶多（Niyata）、特殊；帕達尼（Padani）、用具；阿跋地摩多尼（Avadlm^tani）、受控制（?）；阿特彌（ATMI）、原子性；阿克沙（Aksha）、器官。五大提婆作用（rive Mafc&bJmtas）。」
30-24. 「摩訶不多（Mah&bhutas），即粗大元素，乃地、水、光、氣、乙太五者。據云，地以為載體而助其他四者。水以潤澤而助其他四者。光以催熟而助其他四者。氣以乾燥而助其他四者。乙太以提供空間而助其他四者。」
味道與氣味。水具有四種特性，即聲、觸、色與味。光具有三種特性，即聲、觸與色。風具有二種特性，即聲與觸。乙太具有一種特性，即聲。如此乃五大（MaMbhutas）之說明。
其同義詞為：Bhutas（元素）、Bhuta-viseshas（特殊元素）、Vikaras（變化）、Akrities（種類）、Tanu（皮膚或軀體？）、Vigraha（形狀）、Santa（令人愉悅的）。
普盧瑟阿。253 哥羅（Ghora），可畏者；姆圖阿（MtU?Aa），愚鈍者。如是已述十六種毗迦羅（Vikaras）。普魯沙（Purusha）。

今人問道：「何謂普魯沙（Purusha）？」答曰：「普魯沙無始無終，性微妙，遍一切處，具知覺，離諸質，永恆存在，為見者，為經驗者，非作用者，為境物之知者，無垢淨，不產生。何以名之為普魯沙？以其古老（Purāṇat），以其安住於身體中（Puri sayate），以其充任祭祀主管者（Purohita）之故。」凡此者，自然皆屬想像性的詞源論；我們不難確認，在普魯沙之名中，我們回溯到吠陀的普魯沙——最高神祇之諸多名號之一，與毗濕瓦羯摩（Visvakarman）、金胎（Hiraṇyagarbha）、祭祀主（Prajāpati）等並列。如梵我（Brahman）當被理解為真我（Atman）時，普魯沙亦可能被用於同一力量之神性與人性兩面。惟其普魯沙之多元性，乃為數論哲學（Sāṁkhya-philosophy）所特有。

沒有開始，沒有中間，也沒有結束。』這不是一個非常令人滿意的答案，但它大概只是指我們永遠不會感知到它的開始、中間或結束。它為什麼是微妙的？因為它沒有部分，超越感官。它為什麼無所不在？因為它像天空一樣到達一切，其範圍無限。它為什麼具有知覺？因為它（暫時地）感知快樂、痛苦和煩惱。它為什麼沒有性質？因為善、中性和惡的性質在其中找不到。它為什麼是永恆的？因為它未被製造，也不能被製造。它為什麼是見證者？因為它感知自然（Prakṛiti）的變化。它為什麼是享受者？因為具有知覺性，它（暫時地）感知快樂和痛苦。它為什麼不是行動者？因為它是漠不關心的，沒有諸質（Guṇas）。它為什麼是身體或對象的認知者？因為它認知客體身體的性質。它為什麼是無垢的？因為無論善行還是惡行都不屬於補盧沙（Purusha）。它為什麼不生產？因為它沒有種子，也就是說，它無法生產任何東西。如此，數論派的補盧沙已被描述。

普魯沙（Purusha）的同義詞有：阿特曼（Atman）、自我（Self）、普曼（Pum）、男性（male）、普姆古尼亞（Pumgunia）、一個有生命的男性生物（a male living creature）、克舍特拉格亞（Kshetragwa）、對象或身體的認識者（knower of objects or of the body）、那羅（Nara）、人、卡維（Kavi）、詩人、梵（Brahman）、阿克沙羅（Akshara）、不可毀滅的、普若（Pram）、靈、雅卡（Yaksha）、任何東西、他（He）。

因此二十五種實體已被描述，即八種實質（Prakritis）、十六種變化（Vikaras）及精神（Purusha）。凡知曉此二十五種實體者，無論其處於人生何階段，無論其蓄著纏結髮髻、髮髻或剃髮，皆得解脫，此毫無疑問。此詩句為數論派哲學家所常引用。在此，《原則匯集》（Tattva-samasa）的第一部分似乎已告結束，包含了二十五種原則（Tattvas）的目錄，分為實質（Prakritis）、變化（Vikaras）及精神（Purusha）三個範疇。
補盧沙（Purusha）（主體）。
I 1. 實性（prakṛti）（對象）。未顯現（avyakta）（混沌）。
2. Mahat 或 Buddhi（光與智慧，作為 Samashti，尚未「個別化」）。
3. 自我執著（Ahamkara，主觀化），5 種微質（Tanmatras，薩埵質），10 種感根，器官（羅沙），加上 1 種意根（Manas，心）（微妙元素）。（5 種知根、5 種業根及意根）。
極微（Tanmatras）。覺知根（Buddhindriyas）。作業根（Karmendriyas）。
聲音，薩伯達（Sabda）。聽覺（Srotra），聽覺。舌音入耳。
2. 觸覺，Sparsa。2. Tvafc，觸覺於 2. 用手掌握。
皮膚。
3. 顏色，盧波（Rupa）。3. 眼睛，見。3. 在足中運動，在眼中。
